element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Progressive difficulty with varied enemy types
Finding keys to rescue lobsters, collecting sponges for environmental tasks

element_introduction_order.element: Environmental hazards (oil tankers, radiation, toxic waste)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Using collected objects to plug holes and mitigate hazards

typical_relief_triggers: Safe platform reached after hazardous section
typical_tension_triggers: Enemy encounter requiring strategic bubble use
Environmental hazard introduction (leaking oil tanker, radiation)
Puzzle requiring object collection in dangerous areas
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Alternating puzzle-solving and combat phases with environmental hazards escalating through level progression; described as 'entertaining and challenging'

difficulty_curve.early_game: Safe introduction to platforming, bubble weapon, and basic enemy encounters
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Puzzle complexity increases with object collection requirements; environmental hazards introduced alongside combat
difficulty_curve.late_game: Combined puzzle-solving and hazard navigation; escalating enemy variety and density
notable_spikes: Introduction of environmental hazard mechanics requiring specific collected objects
Levels with multiple simultaneous puzzle and combat demands

notable_level_patterns.description: Twelve levels featuring titles that parody James Bond film names, thematically connecting gameplay to underwater espionage narrative
notable_level_patterns.example_level: License to Bubble (parody of Licence to Kill), A View to a Spill (parody of A View to a Kill), Leak and Let Die (parody of Live and Let Die), From Three Mile Island with Love (parody of From Russia with Love)

notable_level_patterns.description: Levels blend combat against enemies via bubble trapping with environmental puzzle solving using collected objects
Levels featuring specific underwater hazards (leaking oil tankers, radiation, toxic waste) that require targeted object use to progress
